title: Using the Privacy by Design methodology for the 2023 Census | Stats NZ
content:
## Using the Privacy by Design methodology for the 2023 Census
01 November 2022, 3:30pm
Stats NZ will be using the Privacy by Design methodology for the 2023 Census.
We have enhanced the way we manage information privacy by applying the Privacy by Design methodology as a requirement for effectively managing personal information across the Census Programme. This is in addition to the standards we already apply to the privacy, confidentiality, and security of the information we collect through the census.
The Privacy by Design methodology allows us to apply more rigour to how we consider and respect privacy. We are using this methodology to help people feel even more confident about the care we are taking with their information. The methodology embeds privacy management throughout the census lifecycle – from the design phase through to ensuring privacy controls are in place before we release any product to do with the census. It increases awareness of privacy and the appropriate handling of personal information in workstreams across the census programme, meaning that everyone in any team involved in census work knows what respect for privacy looks like and how to apply that respect across their work.
Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data and Census have more information about how we keep data safe and secure. There are seven principles central to the Privacy by Design methodology:
- Proactive not reactive, preventative not remedial
- Privacy as the default
- Privacy embedded into design
- Full functionality – positive-sum, not zero-sum
- End-to-end security – lifecycle protection
- Visibility and transparency
- Respect for user privacy – keep it user-centric.
Privacy by Design (PbD) , the Digital Government webpage, explains these principles in more detail.
To ensure our privacy approach for the 2023 Census is appropriate, we engaged the privacy consultancy company Simply Privacy Ltd to provide an independent review of our planning and processes.
Assurance review on 2023 Census privacy approach provides more information about Simply Privacy’s independent review.
